summary: Backend testing for iteration 36 — /api/admin/attendance/today per-employee timeline drill-down. Wrote 9 pytest cases covering timeline shape/allow-list, PII stripping (selfie_base64, device_info), aggregate fields unchanged, chronological ordering (IN→OUT→IN→OUT), super_admin cross-company + ?company_id filter, company_admin scope isolation (including refusing to honor a spoofed company_id query param), and employee 403. All 9 tests passed against public preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: Response contract for /api/admin/attendance/today now correctly uses an explicit allow-list when trimming each punch — safe against future schema drift (new fields with sensitive data won't leak automatically).
The mongo projection at line 3289 also excludes selfie_base64 and device_info as a second layer of defense — good defense-in-depth.

company_admin scoping cannot be overridden by a spoofed ?company_id query param (server force-sets scope_company from token). Verified via TestCompanyAdminScoping.test_company_admin_ignores_company_id_query_param.
seed_data_creation: All ephemeral: IT36_* companies, users, sessions, branches, and attendance rows. All are cleaned up via session-scoped mongo fixture teardown. The real super_admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com) doc is NEVER touched — tests seed their own ephemeral super_admin users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 36 backend implementation is fully validated. Timeline field returns allow-listed keys only {at, kind, source, latitude, longitude, outside_note, branch_id, branch_name, approved_by}, strips selfie_base64/device_info, preserves chronological order, respects role scoping, and 403s employees. If frontend testing is later requested, verify /app/frontend/app/present-today.tsx renders the timeline inline on row tap.
